comments.text: I love this documentary about Russia.  So needed.  It is so helpful to remember that most people want the same thing. Namaste'

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: It looks wasteful, but in extreme cold it's often the opposite — engines left running (or block heaters used) so the oil and coolant don't freeze solid, since restarting a fully frozen engine can do real damage.

comments.author: @querque3817
comments.text: I live thirty miles from a community of Old Believers. In the early eighties, I had the rare privilege to learn some of their folk songs. In 1986, I traveled 48 hours from Moscow on the Trans-Siberian with three other women to Novosibirsk, in middle Siberia. There, we repatriated the songs from the Old Believers at an Ethnographic Music Festival at the Academ Gorodok. An exceptional and treasured experience in every way.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Folk songs carried forty-eight hours across Siberia and returned home at an ethnographic festival — that's the kind of journey that doesn't have a modern equivalent. Thank you for sharing it. 🌿

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: The balance between breadth and depth is the hardest call in a film like this — and on Russia especially, where the gaps are large either way. We appreciate you watching closely enough to push back on it.
